pages.name: Home
pages.description: Overview page for the AI customer communications platform with quick access to core workflows and guided setup for a single admin user.
primary_functions: Introduce the product value for AI customer communications to B2B visitors
Provide quick links to configure bots, automations, routing, analytics, and integrations
Highlight next steps such as setting up the first website chatbot or starting a trial

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Bots' in the header or a homepage quick link such as 'Set up your first website chatbot'.
Click 'Automations' in the header or a suggested next step to configure urgent email auto-replies.
Click a suggested next step to configure routing rules for pricing vs technical issues.
Click 'Analytics' in the header or an analytics-related quick link.
Click a quick link such as 'Connect Demo CRM Sandbox'.
Click a quick link to create or manage FAQ articles.
Click 'Settings' in the header.
Click a primary CTA such as 'View pricing & start trial'.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Home' link in the main header navigation.
Primary landing URL for the product marketing site (e.g., root domain).

pages.name: Bots
pages.description: Central hub to view and manage all AI bots across channels for the single workspace.
primary_functions: List existing bots with status, channel, and basic performance indicators
Create a new bot by launching the bot builder with a selected channel and template
Open an existing bot in the bot builder for editing and optimization

incoming_params.description: Optional filter to show bots for a specific channel type (e.g., 'website').

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'New Bot', choose 'Website chatbot' and the 'Lead Generation' template.
Click an existing bot row such as 'Website Lead Assistant' to edit it.

access_methods.description: Accessible from the 'Bots' item in the main header navigation.
Linked from quick actions on the Home and Settings Hub pages.
Optional channel_type query parameter can be used to pre-filter bots.

pages.name: Bot Builder
pages.description: Configuration workspace for a single bot, including targeting, messages, behavior, and knowledge base usage.
primary_functions: Set bot basics such as name, channel selection (e.g., Production Website), and template
Configure audience targeting rules, including conditions like country equals United States
Design the conversation flow with sequential questions, required fields, and conditional actions
Configure availability, business hours, timezone, offline messages, and repeat behavior options
Enable knowledge base content as an AI source and select preferred content sources for answers
Activate or deactivate the bot and save configuration changes

incoming_params.description: ID of the bot being edited; use 'new' when creating a new bot such as 'Website Lead Assistant'.
Optional bot type hint (e.g., 'website_chatbot') when creating a new bot.
Optional template name (e.g., 'Lead Generation') pre-selected for a new bot.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to bots' or navigate away after saving or activating the bot.

access_methods.description: Accessed as bot_builder.html?botId={id} for editing or bot_builder.html?botId=new for creation.
Reached from the Bots list and from contextual links (e.g., after enabling KB usage).

pages.name: Automations
pages.description: Listing page for all automation rules and workflows that orchestrate AI-driven replies and other actions.
primary_functions: Display existing automations with status, triggers, and last updated information
Create a new automation by opening the automation editor for detailed configuration
Toggle automations on or off to control whether rules are active

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'New Automation' to create an automation such as the urgent email auto-reply.
Click an existing automation rule row to edit its configuration.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Automations' link in the main header navigation.
Linked from the Home and Settings Hub pages as a quick entry point for automation setup.
Optional statusFilter query parameter used to pre-filter the automations list.

pages.name: Automation Editor
pages.description: Rule builder for defining triggers, conditions, wait steps, and AI-powered email actions.
primary_functions: Configure automation triggers such as new conversation created on specific channels like email
Add conditions based on tags and other attributes (e.g., tags contains 'urgent')
Define actions including generating AI replies, editing subject and body, and setting sender addresses
Insert wait or delay steps (e.g., 10 minutes) before sending responses
Enable automatic sending of AI-generated replies without human review and activate the rule

incoming_params.description: ID of the automation rule to edit; 'new' when creating a new automation such as the urgent auto-reply.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Save' or 'Back to automations' after configuring the rule.

access_methods.description: Accessed as automation_editor.html?automationRuleId={id} for editing or 'new' for creation.
Navigated to from the Automations list when creating or editing a rule.

pages.name: Routing Rules
pages.description: Settings page to define and prioritize message routing and inbox assignment rules such as pricing vs technical issue routing.
primary_functions: Create new routing rules with condition groups based on message text keywords
Assign conversations to specific inboxes such as Sales or Support and set priority levels
Configure evaluation settings such as applying rules only on the first customer message
Set rule schedules (e.g., always on), scopes (e.g., global), and adjust rule order precedence
Activate or deactivate individual routing rules

incoming_params.description: Optional ID of a routing rule to open directly in edit mode.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to Settings' from the routing rules page.

access_methods.description: Accessed from the Settings Hub 'Routing rules' tile and from guided steps on the Home page.
Optional routingRuleId can be provided for deep-linking to a specific rule like 'Pricing vs Technical Routing'.

pages.name: Analytics Dashboard
pages.description: Main analytics view showing pinned AI performance and conversation reports with a customizable layout.
primary_functions: Display pinned analytics widgets including AI performance and conversation metrics
Create new reports by launching the analytics report builder
Reorder dashboard widgets such as moving the 'AI Resolution - 30 days' report to the top row
Pin or unpin existing reports to control visibility on the dashboard

incoming_params.description: Optional report ID to visually highlight after saving or pinning a report.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'New report' from the analytics dashboard.
Click an existing report widget's 'Edit' action.

access_methods.description: Accessible from the 'Analytics' item in the main header navigation.
Optional highlightReportId used when returning from the report builder.

pages.name: Analytics Report Builder
pages.description: Configuration interface for building custom analytics reports such as AI resolution rate by channel.
primary_functions: Select report type (e.g., conversations or AI performance) and choose primary metrics like AI resolution rate
Set date ranges such as the last 30 days and add filters including AI coverage percentage thresholds
Group data by dimensions such as channel (chat, email, WhatsApp) and add secondary metrics
Define sort order (e.g., by AI-resolved conversations in descending order)
Name the report, add descriptions or notes, save it, and pin it to the main dashboard

incoming_params.description: ID of the analytics report to edit; use 'new' to create a new report such as 'AI Resolution - 30 days'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Save' (optionally with 'Pin to dashboard') after configuring the report.

access_methods.description: Accessed as analytics_report_builder.html?reportId={id} for editing or 'new' for creating a report.
Reached from 'New report' and 'Edit report' actions on the Analytics Dashboard.

pages.name: Integrations
pages.description: Overview and configuration center for external integrations such as CRMs, including the Demo CRM Sandbox.
primary_functions: List available integrations including the Demo CRM Sandbox with connection status
Connect to the Demo CRM Sandbox by entering API keys or access tokens and selecting environment (e.g., sandbox)
Configure field mappings between platform contact fields and CRM fields (email, full name, company)
Enable options such as syncing new conversations to the CRM or auto-creating contacts
Save and enable integrations after configuration to activate data sync

incoming_params.description: Optional ID of the integration (e.g., Demo CRM Sandbox) to open directly in configuration view.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to Settings' after configuring an integration.

access_methods.description: Accessed from the Settings Hub integrations card and from Home page quick links.
integrationId indicates which integration to show in detail.

pages.name: Knowledge Base
pages.description: Authoring and management space for help center and FAQ articles that can be used by AI to answer customer questions.
primary_functions: List existing knowledge base articles with their visibility status
Create new articles with titles, content, and public visibility (e.g., pricing, trial, support hours FAQs)
Edit or update article content and visibility to keep information current
Publish or unpublish articles that the AI and customers can reference

incoming_params.description: ID of the knowledge base to manage; defaults to the primary public knowledge base when omitted.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'AI & chatbot settings' or similar within the knowledge base section.
Click 'Use in chatbots' to go configure bots that will use these articles.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Knowledge Base' link in the main header navigation.
Accessible via the 'Knowledge Base' link in the footer as a public help center entry.
knowledgeBaseId selects which knowledge base's articles to show.

pages.name: Knowledge Base Settings
pages.description: Control how the AI uses knowledge base content to answer customer questions across bots and channels.
primary_functions: Enable or disable the option for the AI to use knowledge base articles for answers
Set the preferred answer source ordering, such as 'Knowledge base first, then conversation history'
Apply and save knowledge base settings that affect bots and AI responses across channels

incoming_params.description: ID of the knowledge base whose AI usage settings are being configured.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to articles' after updating AI knowledge base usage.
Click a contextual link such as 'Configure bots to use this knowledge base'.

access_methods.description: Reached from the Knowledge Base page or the Settings Hub under AI/Knowledge Base configuration.
knowledgeBaseId identifies which knowledge base settings to edit.

pages.name: Users & Team
pages.description: User management page for inviting and configuring support agents and their inbox access.
primary_functions: View the list of users and their roles within the workspace
Invite a new user by specifying full name, email, and role such as Agent
Assign or restrict inbox access so a user can only access specific inboxes like Support
Configure user permissions such as limiting access to assigned inboxes only
Set custom working hours for each user including specific weekdays and time ranges

incoming_params.description: Optional email address to highlight in the user list, e.g., after inviting alex.support@example.test.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'View Support inbox settings' after assigning a user to the Support inbox.
Click 'Back to Settings' from the Users & Team page.

access_methods.description: Accessed from the Settings Hub 'Users & Team' card.
Optional highlightEmail used to focus on a specific user after navigation.

pages.name: Inbox Settings
pages.description: Configuration page for an individual inbox such as the Support inbox, including team membership.
primary_functions: Select an inbox (e.g., Support) to view its specific settings and details
Manage team members assigned to the inbox by checking or unchecking user access
Review related inbox options such as default priority, notifications, or routing context

incoming_params.description: ID of the inbox to configure, e.g., the Support inbox.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to team' after adjusting team members for an inbox.
Click 'Back to Settings' from the Inbox Settings page.

access_methods.description: Accessed as inbox_settings.html?inboxId={id} to configure a specific inbox.
Linked from Users & Team and Settings Hub pages when managing inboxes.

pages.name: Pricing
pages.description: Pricing overview with plan comparison and entry point to start a trial on the appropriate AI-enabled plan.
primary_functions: Toggle between monthly and annual billing views and display equivalent monthly prices
Compare plans on price, included AI features, and number of seats or teammates
Identify plans under a specific monthly equivalent price threshold that include AI and sufficient seats
Start a trial for a selected plan by opening the trial signup form

incoming_params.description: Billing type view to display: 'monthly' or 'annual'; passed through to getPricingPlans.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Start trial' or 'Get started' on a plan after startTrialForPlan successfully creates a trial signup.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Pricing' link in the main header navigation.
Accessible via the 'Pricing' link in the footer for visitors comparing plans.
billing query parameter determines which billing view (monthly/annual) to show.

pages.name: Trial Signup
pages.description: Form for starting a trial of a selected pricing plan, capturing company and contact details.
primary_functions: Display the selected plan details from pricing and confirm billing type (e.g., annual)
Collect company information such as company name and company size range
Capture contact details including work email and job title or role
Allow the user to enable AI features via a checkbox such as 'Use AI assistant'
Submit the form to create the account or begin the trial for the chosen plan

incoming_params.description: ID of the trial signup record created via startTrialForPlan; used when submitting the signup form.
ID of the selected pricing plan to display on the signup form.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Continue' or after successfully submitting the trial signup form to enter the app.

access_methods.description: Accessed after selecting a plan on the Pricing page, which passes trialSignupId and planId in the URL.
Requires trialSignupId and planId parameters to associate form submission with the correct trial and plan.

pages.name: Settings Hub
pages.description: Central hub for navigating to all configuration sections of the workspace and viewing high-level setup status.
primary_functions: Provide shortcuts to settings subsections such as routing rules, integrations, users, and inbox settings
Link to knowledge base settings for AI answer configuration
Summarize key configuration status indicators (e.g., routing enabled, CRM connected, KB for AI on)

incoming_params.description: Optional section identifier (e.g., 'routing', 'integrations', 'users', 'inboxes', 'knowledge_base') to highlight a specific settings tile.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Routing rules' card in the Settings Hub.
Click the 'Demo CRM Sandbox' integration shortcut.
Click the 'Users & Team' card.
Click a shortcut to configure the Support inbox.
Click 'Knowledge Base AI settings' in the Settings Hub.
Click a quick link to manage bots from the Settings Hub.
Click a quick link to manage automations from the Settings Hub.
Click a quick link to manage knowledge base articles from the Settings Hub.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Settings' link in the main header navigation.
section parameter used to deep-link to highlight a specific settings area.

pages.name: About
pages.description: Background information about the company and product, including mission, story, and customer proof.
primary_functions: Present the company mission and story behind the AI customer communications platform
Highlight credibility indicators such as customers served, testimonials, or certifications

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a CTA such as 'Talk to our team' from the About page.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'About' link in the footer.
Can be linked from external sites or marketing campaigns as an about-us page.

pages.name: Contact
pages.description: Contact information and inquiry form for prospects and customers to reach Sales or Support.
primary_functions: Display contact details such as email addresses, office locations, and support channels
Provide a simple contact form for sales or general inquiries

incoming_params.description: Optional pre-selected topic for the contact form (e.g., 'Sales', 'Support').

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Contact' link in the footer.
Can be linked directly from marketing emails or external properties.
topic parameter can prefill the inquiry topic in the contact form.

header_links.description: Homepage and starting point for key workflows
Manage and configure AI bots and chatbots
View and create automation rules and workflows
Analytics dashboard with AI performance reports
Create and manage FAQ articles for AI answers
Settings hub for routing, integrations, users, and inboxes
Pricing plans and trials for the AI platform

footer_links.description: Learn more about the company and product
Public help center and FAQ articles
View pricing plans and start a trial
